perturbation.original_text: 11. The parties agree that for this agreement Germa n law applies excluding the United Nations Conventi on on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. The legal venue for all disputes arising from this agre ement is the head office of the company Helukabel in Hemmingen.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The confidentiality agreement does not apply to statements of the company Helukabel in its catalogu es, datasheets and on the homepage and also not to info rmation which a) the receiving party was already aware of or whic h was developed by the disclosing party itself inde pendent of the disclosure, b) is publicly known without this agreement having been broken, c) was received by a third party without infringeme nt of this or any other confidentiality agreement. 11. The parties agree that for this agreement Germa n law applies excluding the United Nations Conventi on on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. The legal venue for all disputes arising from this agre ement is the head office of the company Helukabel in Hemmingen.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law and venue selection clause to paragraph 6, which discusses exceptions to the confidentiality agreement, obscures its importance and may lead a party to overlook it. Governing law and venue are fundamental aspects that require specific sections in a contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 307 BGB (German Civil Code) deals with the control of the content of standard business terms. While this clause itself might not be deemed unfair per se, embedding it within a paragraph detailing exceptions to the confidentiality agreement could be interpreted as an attempt to obscure or weaken its effect, potentially leading to a finding of unfair surprise under German law, especially if it disadvantages the weaker party. This is because the location of the clause does not adequately highlight its significance.

(2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... merken § 307 Inhaltskontrolle (1) 1 Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. 2 Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist. (2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflich
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the governing law and venue selection clause to paragraph 6, which discusses exceptions to the confidentiality agreement, obscures its importance and may lead a party to overlook it, and that governing law and venue are fundamental aspects that require specific sections in a contract. The law explanation says that embedding this clause within a paragraph detailing exceptions to the confidentiality agreement could be interpreted as an attempt to obscure or weaken its effect, potentially leading to a finding of unfair surprise under German law, especially if it disadvantages the weaker party. The definition of structural flaws leading to legal contradiction says that the contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. § 307 BGB, as provided in the scraped snippet 2, states that "Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen." The modified contract text does exactly this, as it buries the governing law clause in a paragraph about confidentiality exceptions, disadvantaging the contract partner. Thus, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This agreement becomes effective with the signat ures of both parties and is valid for three years. It is tacitly extended by one year if it is not terminated three months before its expiration.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The contract parties are obligated to preclude d isclosure of confidential information to third part ies. The parties will entrust confidential information solely to those em ployees, consultants and third party companies whi ch are concerned with the processing of the order and whic h are legally or contractually obligated to secrecy . 8. This agreement becomes effective with the signat ures of both parties and is valid for three years. It is tacitly extended by one year if it is not terminated three months before its expiration.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause specifying the agreement's effective date and term length to paragraph 3, which concerns non-disclosure to third parties, obscures the fundamental terms of the contract's duration and renewal process. The effective date and duration are typically stated at the end of a contract, not buried amidst obligations regarding third parties.
perturbation.law_explanation: BGB § 133 (German Civil Code) requires the interpretation of declarations of intent based on the true intention of the declaring party, not merely the literal meaning of the words. Hiding the duration and effective date within a clause about third-party disclosure can make it more difficult to ascertain the parties' true intention regarding the contract's term, especially in cases of tacit renewal, potentially leading to disputes and challenging the enforceability of the renewal provision.

perturbation.justification: The structural flaw, specifically the placement of the agreement's effective date and term length within a section concerning non-disclosure, obscures the parties' intentions regarding the contract's duration. According to the Law Explanation and BGB § 133 (provided in Scraped Law Snippet 2), the interpretation of declarations of intent should be based on the true intention of the declaring party. Burying key terms weakens their visibility, making it more difficult to ascertain the parties' true intention and therefore contradicts BGB § 133. The Perturbation Explanation supports this by stating that such placement obscures the contract's duration and renewal process.

perturbation.original_text: 7. If the recipient is forced to expose the confide ntial information due to a legal regulation, a lega lly valid judgement or a non-disputable official decision or decree in part or in whole, it shall immediately inform the o wner of the confidential information in writing so that the own er can take measures to protect the confidential in formation.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Every contract party can disclose confidential i nformation to the other contract party orally or in writing. If the disclosure is in writing, the disclosing party will mark the confidential documents with the additiona l terms “Confidential” or “Liable to Secrecy” so that it is apparent to the other party which information is e specially in need to protection. 7. If the recipient is forced to expose the confide ntial information due to a legal regulation, a lega lly valid judgement or a non-disputable official decision or decree in part or in whole, it shall immediately inform the o wner of the confidential information in writing so that the own er can take measures to protect the confidential in formation.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the mandatory disclosure regarding legally compelled disclosure to paragraph 2 which discusses initial voluntary disclosure. This placement obscures the importance of this clause. Usually, clauses describing what to do if compelled by law have to be clearly visible and in their own section.
perturbation.law_explanation: GeschGehG § 5 (German Trade Secrets Act) governs the protection of trade secrets. By placing the clause related to mandatory disclosure due to legal requirements within a section about voluntary disclosure practices, it makes it less obvious and easily overlooked. This impacts the balance, because the recipient is under a mandate to inform the owner of the trade secrets if compelled to disclose the trade secret. This can contradict with GeschGehG § 5, which outlines measures that can be taken to protect trade secrets.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 5 GeschGehG Ausnahmen Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen buzer.de Bundesrecht - tagaktuell konsolidiert - alle Fassungen seit 2006 Vorschriftensuche § / Artikel Gesetz Volltextsuche nur in GeschGehG ↑ nach oben ↓ nach unten Start Suchen Sachgebiete Aktuell Verkündet Über buzer.de Qualität Kontakt Datenschutz Impressum Tools: Blog-Plugin Mobilversion Vorschau Update via: Web-Widget Feed Rechtskataster Support Buzer.de Werben auf buzer.de Sie sind hier: Start > Inhaltsverzeichnis GeschGehG > § 5 Mail bei Änderungen § 5 - Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) Artikel 1 G. v. 18.04.2019 BGBl. I S. 466 ( Nr. 13 ) Geltung ab 26.04.2019; FNA: 427-1 Gewerblicher Rechtsschutz 1 weitere Fassung | Drucksachen / Entwurf / Begründung | wird in 29 Vorschriften zitiert Abschnitt 1 Allgemeines § 4 ← → § 6 § 5 Ausnahmen § 5 wird in 1 Vorschrift zitiert Die Erlangung, die Nutzung oder die Offenlegung eines Geschäftsgeheimnisses fällt nicht unter die Verbote des § 4 , wenn dies zum Schutz eines berechtigten Interesses erfolgt, insbesondere 1. zur Ausübung des Rechts der freien Meinungsäußerung und der Informationsfreiheit, einschließlich der Achtung der Freiheit und der Pluralität der Medien; 2. zur Aufdeckung einer rechtswidrigen Handlung oder eines beruflichen oder sonstigen Fehlverhaltens, wenn die Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung geeignet ist, das allgemeine öffentliche Interesse zu schützen; 3. im Rahmen der Offenlegung durch Arbeitnehmer gegenüber der Arbeitnehmervertretung, wenn dies erforderlich ist, damit die Arbeitnehmervertretung ihre Aufgaben erfüllen kann. § 4 ← → § 6 GeschGehG - Änderungen überwachen Sie werden über jede verkündete oder in Kraft tretende Änderung per Mail informiert, sofort, wöchentlich oder in dem Intervall, das Sie gewählt haben. Auf Wunsch werden Sie zusätzlich im konfigurierten Abstand vor Inkrafttreten erinnert. Stellen Sie Ihr Paket zu überwachender Vorschriften beliebig zusammen. Rechtskataster - Jetzt anmelden! Weiter
perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves relocating a clause regarding mandatory disclosure due to legal compulsion into a section about voluntary disclosure practices. This obscures the importance of the legally compelled disclosure. The Law Explanation states this impacts the balance, because the recipient is under a mandate to inform the owner of the trade secrets if compelled to disclose the trade secret. The Perturbation Explanation states clauses describing what to do if compelled by law have to be clearly visible and in their own section. The changed text violates the principle that legally required disclosures should be clearly presented. Scraped Law Snippet 2 references § 5 GeschGehG which outlines exceptions. The modified contract makes it more likely that the mandatory disclosure will be overlooked, potentially undermining the trade secret protection by not adequately informing the owner of the disclosure. Therefore, a structural flaw creates a legal contradiction because a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section.
